items.text:
Subject: Fall Room Inspection Scheduled

Dear Ms. Okafor,

Residence Life will carry out its annual safety inspection of every room in Kingsley Hall on Wednesday, October 14, between 9:00 AM and 3:00 PM. You do not need to be present, but please make sure your smoke detector is unblocked and that all walkways are clear of boxes and cords.

If you would rather attend in person, reply to this message by October 10 to reserve a specific time slot. Residents who lock or block access on inspection day, and who have not arranged an alternate appointment, will be charged a $25 re-inspection fee.

Inspection results will be posted to your housing portal within three business days.

Thank you for your cooperation,
Marcus Reed, Residence Coordinator

questions.stem: According to the email, what should residents do to prepare their rooms?

options.A: To remove all furniture from the room
options.B: To keep the smoke detector unblocked
options.C: To lock the room during the inspection
options.D: To post the results on the portal

questions.explanation: The email asks residents to make sure the smoke detector is unblocked and walkways are clear before the inspection.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who blocks access without an appointment?

options.A: They will be charged an extra fee
options.B: They will be assigned a new room
options.C: They will be inspected the next year
options.D: They will lose their portal account

questions.explanation: Residents who block access without arranging an alternate appointment will be charged a $25 re-inspection fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the inspection results?

options.A: They are mailed to each resident's home
options.B: They are announced at a hall meeting
options.C: They are posted online after several days
options.D: They are shared only with parents

questions.explanation: The results are posted to the housing portal within three business days, so residents can view them online shortly after.
